text.title: llamaindex-hybrid-retrieval Core Rules
text.summary: Rules for LlamaIndex hybrid retrieval: QueryFusionRetriever, cross-encoder reranking, AutoMergingRetriever, similarity thresholding.

rule.statement: Use QueryFusionRetriever(mode='reciprocal_rerank') to combine ≥2 retrievers (vector + BM25). Set num_queries=1-4 based on rerank budget.
rule.rationale: RRF is more stable than weighted sum; num_queries beyond 4 burns LLM calls for diminishing returns.

rule.statement: Pre-rerank pool size (fusion_top_k) must be at least 4x the post-rerank size (rerank_top_n). A rerank over 3 candidates is a no-op.
rule.rationale: Rerank needs a real candidate set to discriminate; tiny pools defeat the purpose.

rule.statement: Apply SimilarityPostprocessor BEFORE the cross-encoder rerank to pre-filter low-relevance candidates. Run rerank on the survivors only.
rule.rationale: Pre-filtering cuts rerank cost 3-5x without recall loss when threshold is calibrated.

rule.statement: Calibrate similarity_cutoff threshold on your own eval set per-query-class. Never reuse a threshold from a different corpus or benchmark.
rule.rationale: Thresholds do not transfer; cross-corpus reuse silently drops recall on niche queries.

rule.statement: Cap QueryFusionRetriever concurrency with num_workers=4-8 or wrap in asyncio.Semaphore(8). Default is unbounded.
rule.rationale: Unbounded fusion opens N times k concurrent LLM calls — straight into rate-limit cliffs.

rule.statement: For hierarchical corpora, wire AutoMergingRetriever to promote a parent node when ≥half its children match.
rule.rationale: AutoMerging is designed for tree-structured corpora; using it on flat corpora adds latency without lift.

rule.statement: Choose a distilled cross-encoder (e.g., ms-marco-MiniLM-L-6-v2) for latency-sensitive deployments; reserve full BERT-class rerankers for offline pipelines.
rule.rationale: Distilled rerankers run sub-100ms on CPU; full BERT crosses 500ms.

rule.statement: Always A/B hybrid vs vector-only on a labeled eval set before shipping. Hybrid adds latency and complexity; the lift must be measurable.
rule.rationale: Without an eval set you cannot justify hybrid's added latency cost.

rule.statement: Stub rule for conclusion 'r-advanced' referenced from 06-decision-tree.xml. Replace with the real testable rule that resolves this branch of the tree.
rule.rationale: Auto-added by fix-methodology-phase-d.py to keep tree refs resolvable.

rule.statement: Stub rule for conclusion 'r-default-high' referenced from 06-decision-tree.xml. Replace with the real testable rule that resolves this branch of the tree.
rule.rationale: Auto-added by fix-methodology-phase-d.py to keep tree refs resolvable.

rule.statement: Stub rule for conclusion 'r-default-low' referenced from 06-decision-tree.xml. Replace with the real testable rule that resolves this branch of the tree.
rule.rationale: Auto-added by fix-methodology-phase-d.py to keep tree refs resolvable.
